title: Avoid Ignoring Return Values from Functions Without Side Effects (Java)
description: When the call to a function doesn't have any side effects, what is the point of making the call if the results are ignored? In such case, either the function call is useless and should be dropped or the source code doesn't behave as expected.
recommendation: Either remove the function call or use the returned value.
impact: Improves code readability and maintainability, and prevents potential logic errors.

checks.message: Either remove the function call or use the returned value.
